case_title: CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA) N.A VS. FLORENTINA SCHENDEL

docket_entries.description: CRC 3.740 REVIEW
DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF DEFAULT (1033) (TRANSACTION ID # 100007364) FILED BY PLAINTIFF CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA) N.A
ABSTRACT OF JUDGMENT ISSUED TO PLAINTIFF CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA) N.A
ORDER DETERMINING CLAIM OF EXEMPTION

LAW AND MOTION 302, HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION OF DEFENDANT FLORENTINA SCHENDEL IS CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 14, 2018 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPT. 302 TO REVIEW THE POSSIBLE ENDING OF THE WAGE GARNISHMENT PROCEEDING AND POSSIBLE RESOLUTION. THE DEFENDANT IS WILLING TO PAY $200.00 PER PAY PERIOD. THE SHERIFF IS DIRECTED TO RELEASE ANY EARNINGS HELD TO THE JUDGMENT CREDITOR FOR THE PAYMENT OF THE JUDGMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185 =302/HK
MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-14-2018 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION OF FLORENTINA SCHENDEL. THE COURT ELIMINATES WAGE GARNISHMENT ON THE CONDITION THAT THE DEFENDANT WILL VOLUNTARILY SEND $200 PER PAY PERIOD TO THE ATTORNEY FOR THE JUDGMENT CREDITOR. IF AT ANYTIME, THE DEFENDANT STOPS VOLUNTARY PAYMENT, THE JUDGMENT CREDITOR CAN ASK THAT IT BE REINSTATED AND ANYTHING THAT THE DEFENDANT HAS NOT PAID WILL BE REQUIRED TO BE PAID. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

calendar.matters:
HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Nov-14-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Florentina Schendel, 415-271-2220 Defendant in Pro Per.
No other Appearance.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant reports status and inquires how to remove wage garnishment and pay the Plaintiff directly. Defendant argues good cause to remove or reduce wage garnishment so as not to impair her credit.

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION OF FLORENTINA SCHENDEL.

The Court eliminates wage garnishment on the condition that the Defendant will voluntarily send $200 per pay period to the attorney for the Judgment Creditor. If at anytime, the Defendant stops voluntary payment, the Judgment Creditor can ask that it be reinstated and anything that the Defendant has not paid will be required to be paid.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Oct-10-2018 Continued To Nov-14-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Vivian Leung Lerche, Tel. No. 1-415-680-5731 specially appearing
for the Plaintiff;
Florentina Schendel, Defendant appearing in pro per.

RULING - HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION OF DEFENDANT FLORENTINA SCHENDEL is continued to November 14, 2018 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302 to review the possible ending of the wage garnishment proceeding and possible resolution. The defendant is willing to pay $200.00 per pay period. The sheriff is directed to release any earnings held to the judgment creditor for the payment of the judgment. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185 =302/hk
